chapter.label: Tale 190
chapter.title: The miracle of some wood given to 192. THE STORY OF A MONK.

paragraphs.text: ABBA BROCHA, THE EGYPTIAN OF THE MONASTERY OF THE GODLY POPE GREGORY, Athanasios the Egyptian, who was connected with the civil author- 4 AND OF HOW HE WAS ABSOLVED ity,” said that Abba Brocha found a spot in the wilderness outside OF EXCOMMUNICATION AFTER DEATH* the city of Seleucia near Antioch and tried to build a small cell . there. As his building progressed, he wanted wood to build the roof. A priest named Peter, coming from Rome, told us concerning the One day he went into the city and found Anatolios, known as ‘the saintly Gregory, pope of that city, that when he became pope, he hunchback’, a magnate of Seleucia, sitting ouiside his house. He built a large monastery for men, and made a rule that none of the went up to him and said: ‘OF your charity, give me a little wood to monks should have anything of his own whatsoever, not cven an roof my house with. The magnate replied testily. ‘Look, there is obo]. There was a brother there who had a brother in the world. To wood over there; take it, and go’, and be indicated a Jarge mast him he appealed, saying: ‘I have no shirt. Of your charity, buy me which he had lying in front of his house and which te had made for Lone.” The brother in secular life said to him: ‘Here are three pieces a vessel of fifty-thousand bushels. Abba Brochas said: ‘<The Lord> of gold; take them and buy yourself whatever you like.” The monk bless you; I will take it’. Still in a bad humour, Anatolios said: took the three pieces of goid, then went and reported it to his ‘Blessed be God’. <The elder> grasped the mast, lifted it from the ‘higoumen, who, when he heard it, went and reported it to the most ground all by himseil and put it on bis shoulders. In this way be holy pope. When the blessed Gregory heard it, he excluded the took it away to his cell. Anatolios was so taken aback by this brother from communion because he had contravened the rule of extraordinary miracle that he granted him as much wood as he the monastery. A little while later, the excluded brother died and the required for his needs. With this, Abba Brocha was able not only to pope did not learn about it. Two or three days Jater the higoumen roof his cell of which we spoke, but to do many other things for his came and reported to him that the brother had gone to <his> rest. monastery.
